Ah! The ONLY occurrences are in the javascript logic! So the HTML markup (with `id="weftBefore"`) is indeed COMPLETELY MISSING from the body! This is exactly why the page is "trá»‘ng trÆ¡n" (empty) â€“ because the HTML markup is completely gone! Wait, let's look at `Index_GAS.html` to find out how the HTML markup was set up. We know it starts at `` on line 975. Let's see if we can copy the HTML markup for both: 1. `` (lines 783 to 802) 2. `` (lines 804 to 973) 3. `` (lines 975 to 1183) Wait! Let's get the exact code for ``. Since the file has a huge base64 logo image from line 984 to line 1180 or so, wait, how many lines is that base64 image? Let's look at the line numbers: - Line 981: `` - Line 982: `
` - Line 984: `FABRIC SHRINKAGE & APPEARANCE TEST REPORT"}` Wait, so the image tag ends *before* line 988! Wait, but the output from `view_file` for `Index_GAS.html` was truncated! Why? Ah, because the base64 string on line 984 is *very long* on a single line! The total line length is hundreds of thousands of characters! That's why it was truncated! Wait! Can we use the base64 image in `fabric_testing.html` currently? Let's check if the base64 image is already inside `fabric_testing.html`. Wait, let's search if `base64` is in `fabric_testing.html` or if `logo-img` is in `fabric_testing.html`. Let's do a grep search. This is very clean.
